title: `PodName=` default value mismatch between documentation and actual behavior
body:
### Issue Description

The default value for `PodName=` is `systemd-%N` according to the documentation:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/400a9a528bfc33d23725958f379cb2bfecb4865e/docs/source/markdown/podman-systemd.unit.5.md?plain=1#L1142

but that does not match the actual behavior of Podman.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Log in to Fedora 42 system as a non-root user
2. Run command
   ```
   mkdir -p ~/.config/containers/systemd
   ```
3. Run command
   ```
   cat << EOF > .config/containers/systemd/test1.pod
   [Pod]
   PodName=systemd-%N
   EOF
   ```
4. Run command
   ```
   cat << EOF > .config/containers/systemd/test2.pod
   [Pod]
   EOF
   ```
5. Run command
   ```
   systemctl --user daemon-reload
   ```
6. Run command
   ```
   systemctl --user start test1-pod.service
   ```
7. Run command
   ```
   systemctl --user start test2-pod.service
   ```
8. Run command
   ```
   podman pod ls
   ```
   The following output is shown
   ```
   POD ID        NAME                STATUS      CREATED         INFRA ID      # OF CONTAINERS
   0df7d2c9296d  systemd-test2      Running     3 seconds ago   1f6db97e5560  1
   5f5ebe41c494  systemd-test1-pod  Running     6 seconds ago   6599edea31d5  1
   ```




### Describe the results you received

In step 8 the generated pod names are `systemd-test1-pod` and `systemd-test2`

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the pod names to follow the same naming scheme.

### podman info output

### Privileged Or Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

### Additional information

comments.body: @ygalblum I assume we should correct the documentation here, and leave the behavior the same?
We discussed the name thing (without seeing the doc) a few days ago: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26007#discussion_r2068799201. @Luap99 WDYT? Should we fix the doc or the code?

> Should we fix the doc or the code?

I think some consistency with containers would be slightly better so I lean towards fixing the code but not by much, so if you like to fix the docs that is totally good with me as well.
